h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’ve ticked off slope, load, surface, and edge strip, so I was hoping this was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, my checked-boxes-for-sign-off line was too simple; it hid whether the ramp still makes the person solve the sideways entry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will proceed with the lobby-queue test to check for physical failures and decide on the sign-off based on those results.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Under failure mode, use the ramp’s evidence: lateral entry with a walker may catch at the edge strip, drop a wheel, force a correction, or break the travel line.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's uncertainty about writing a non-vague failure mode by providing concrete, observable criteria based on the ramp's physical evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Make “small” visible. Watch whether the ramp causes the change: wheel angle, pause, lift, widened turn, hand signal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's anxiety about identifying a 'small correction' by breaking down exactly what physical behaviors constitute a small correction.
